heroHeadline: Iran Sports Minister: Participation 'Very Low' Without Security Guarantees; EU Commission Reviewing FIFA Ticket Complaint — 66 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: Iran Sports Minister Donyamali confirmed on April 5 that the government's relocation request is 'still valid' while offering a conditional opening — participation is possible 'if security guarantees are provided'; Bloomberg analysis on April 6 concludes relocating Iran's matches is 'extraordinarily difficult' and unprecedented; the FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 is the key decision point 24 days away. The European Commission confirmed receipt of the FSE/Euroconsumers anti-trust complaint and will assess it under standard procedures. USMNT forward Patrick Agyemang was stretchered off at Derby County with a suspected ankle/Achilles injury, complicating Pochettino's May 26 roster call. FIFA's final venue inspection tour runs April 14–28 across all 16 host cities.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune
